_about: RAG eval set. Each item is a (question, expected) pair. Run with: python scripts/run_eval.py

_schema.question: the user's natural-language question to send to the retriever
_schema.filters: optional dict — same shape as the chat /chat 'filters' payload (source_type, app_code, tags). Leave out for no filter.
_schema.expected_doc_ids: list of DocRecord ids that count as a hit (any match in top-N = hit)
_schema.expected_filenames: list of filename substrings (case-insensitive); any retrieved chunk whose file contains one = hit
_schema.expected_keywords: list of phrases that should appear in the retrieved chunk text (case-insensitive); any match = hit
_schema.notes: free-form context for the human author

_tips: Use 'expected_filenames' for cases where you know which doc should be cited but don't have the doc_id.
Use 'expected_keywords' for cases where the exact source doesn't matter but the answer text should mention specific terms.
All three expected_* fields are ORed — any retrieved chunk in the top-N satisfying any of them counts as a hit.
Aim for ~30 questions covering different types: factoid (identifier lookups), procedural ('how do I…'), conceptual ('why does X work this way'), comparative ('what changed between v1 and v2').

items.question: How do I refund a payment?
items.notes: Procedural query about billing-svc refund flow. Replace this example with a real one from your corpus.

items.question: What is error code AUTH-410?
items.notes: Identifier lookup — BM25 should win this strongly. Good signal that the keyword side of fusion is healthy.

items.question: Why does the system use OAuth tokens?
items.notes: Conceptual / how-does-X-work query. HyDE-style approaches help here.

items.question: What's different between v1 and v2 of token issuance?
items.notes: Cross-version question. With APP_VERSION_MAP, only v2 manuals may come back unless v1 is also in the active policy.
